content:
# Agent Teams Adapter RFC: Post-Review Status

**Date:** 2026-03-28
**Doc:** repos/corvia/docs/rfcs/2026-03-28-agent-teams-adapter-design.md
**Status:** Draft, reviewed by 5 personas, fixes applied

## Review Summary
5 reviewers (Senior SWE, PM, QA, Security, Integration Architect) all said "Ready to implement with fixes" at High confidence. No critical blockers.

## Fixes Applied (12 total)
1. Hook security baseline: input validation, path traversal prevention, umask 077, atomic writes
2. Concurrent write protection: flock for tasks.jsonl, atomic rename for file copies
3. SessionEnd sweep scoped to relevant team only, wrapped in error handler
4. Conditional hook installation (only if Agent Teams enabled)
5. Multi-domain dispatch: source_path based routing, two corvia.toml entries
6. Team end timestamp + status (completed/abandoned) in team structure entry
7. content_role: "task" not "plan" for task entries
8. assigned_to edge points to teammate session entry (not team)
9. Added ran_during edge (team lifecycle, distinct from has_member)
10. Deferred edge wiring via .pending-edges for adapter ordering independence
11. Broadcast message dedup: hash(sender+content+timestamp)
12. Expanded test plan: 8 -> 16 tests with fixtures

## Ready for Implementation
All reviewer concerns addressed. The RFC is ready for Phase 1 (hooks) implementation.
